DMX Controller App

Flexible lighting control for live performances

A desktop application for controlling DMX lighting fixtures with support for Serial DMX, sACN/E1.31, and WLED output protocols. Each output supports a full 512-channel DMX universe, with unlimited sACN universes over your network. Import fixtures using the industry-standard GDTF format, sync effects to music with tap tempo, and control everything live with MIDI controllers. Perform live with an intuitive tile-based grid interface.

This software is free and will always be free.

Download for Desktop View on GitHub

Note: This project is maintained by one person and is under active development. If you encounter bugs or have feature requests, please file an issue on GitHub.

Getting Started

Run your first live light show in five steps:

  1. Download & Install

    Download the latest release for your platform from GitHub Releases. Available for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

  2. Create a Patch

    Navigate to the Patch page to configure your outputs and fixtures.

    Add an output for your hardware:

    • Serial DMX: Click "+", select "Serial", and choose your USB-DMX adapter from the dropdown. The port will auto-reconnect when available.
    • sACN/E1.31: Click "+", select "sACN", and enter your receiver's IP address. Configure the universe number as needed.
    • WLED: Click "+", select "WLED", enter your device's IP address, and click "Sync" to auto-discover segments.

    For DMX outputs, import fixture profiles by dragging GDTF files into the Fixture Profiles panel, or create custom profiles manually. Download profiles for thousands of fixtures from gdtf-share.com.

    Patch your fixtures by dragging them from the Fixture Profiles panel into the universe visualizer. Group similar fixtures together to control them as a unit.

  3. Add a Tile to the Live View

    Go to the Live page and click on a blank tile to configure it. Tiles are your control surface for triggering effects during a performance.

  4. Create a Ramp Effect

    Click the "+" button to add a Ramp effect. Select your fixture group as the target, set the start and end colors or intensities, and configure the duration in beats. The effect will smoothly transition between states.

  5. Sync the Beat & Enjoy

    Use Tap Tempo to sync the beat to your music. Tap your tiles to trigger effects and watch your lights come alive!

System Requirements

  • Windows 10+, macOS 10.15+, or Debian Linux
  • USB-DMX adapter for serial output (optional)
  • Network connection for sACN/WLED output (optional)
  • USB-MIDI controller for external input (optional)

Made with ❤️ by Will Martin
